assertions.text: Output contains at least 2 separate markdown tables (not everything crammed into one table)
assertions.evidence: The response contains two distinct markdown tables: 'Cancellation Refund Eligibility' and 'Refund Amount (when refund is given)'

assertions.text: The tables cover distinct concerns — e.g. pricing/plans is separate from cancellation/refund rules
assertions.evidence: First table covers eligibility (whether a refund is given), second table covers the refund amount calculation — explicitly noted as 'two distinct concerns worth separating: whether a refund is given (the interesting rule), and how much (arithmetic)'

assertions.text: At least one output column in each table ends with '?'
assertions.evidence: First table has 'Refund Given?' column; second table has 'Refund?' column — both output columns end with '?'

assertions.text: The 24-hour no-refund exception is included as a distinct scenario row
assertions.evidence: Row 'Cancels just inside 24h window | Monthly | 12 | 1 | no | Within 24h of renewal' explicitly covers the 24-hour no-refund exception as a distinct scenario

assertions.text: Column names use domain/business language — no implementation terms like 'boolean', 'int', 'isProrated', camelCase identifiers
assertions.evidence: Columns are named: 'Scenario', 'Plan', 'Hours Since Most Recent Charge', 'Days into Cycle', 'Refund Given?', 'Reason?', 'Cycle Length', 'Days Remaining', 'Plan Price', 'Refund?' — all business/domain language with no camelCase or implementation terms
